revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天種子命]]
{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Usami no Mikoto|qid=Q110796060|1=Usami no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Ōhimoro-no-mikoto|qid=Q110796412|1=Ōhimoro-no-mikoto}}|father={{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|qid=Q91088537|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}; {{ill|Q106107009|qid=Q106107009|1=Amanami no Mikoto}}}}


 


[[Amenotaneko]] was the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He was an ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan]]
{{Infobox noble (Japan)|name = Amenotane no Mikoto
| image = 
| image_size = 
| caption = 
| era = {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, Ancient times
| birth_date = Unknown
| death_date = Unknown
| other_names = Amenotaneko no Mikoto, Amenotaneki no Mikoto
| posthumous_name = 
| divine_name = 
| religious_name = 
| resting_place = 
| court_Rank = Nakatomi
| lord = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}
| clan = Ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|qid=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi-no-Muraji|1=Nakatomi clan}}
| parents = Father: {{ill|Ame no Oshikumo ne no Mikoto|ja|天押雲根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Oshikumo ne no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q91088537}}
| siblings =
| wife = Usatsuhime no Mikoto
| children = Usaomi no Mikoto
| special_notes = 
}}
'''Amenotane no Mikoto''' (天種子命, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japanese|1=Japan}} noble who served as the ancestor of the '''{{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|qid=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi-no-Muraji|1=Nakatomi clan}}'''. He is also known as '''Amenotaneko no Mikoto''' and '''Amenotaneki no Mikoto'''<ref>『{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|en|Sonpi Bunmyaku|qid=Q1704738|lt=Sonpi Bunmyaku|1=Sonpi Bunmyaku}}』</ref>.
== Overview ==
He appears only once in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} during the section on the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|qid=Q11590444|lt=Jinmu Tōsei|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}}. When {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} reached {{ill|Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa|ja|宇佐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa|qq=}} in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|qid=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province|1=Tsukushi Province}}, {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|lt=Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|lt=Usatsuhiko|ja|菟狭津彦|lt=Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|lt=Usatsuhiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 菟狭津彦命}} and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐国造|lt=Usa Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, served him by building a temporary palace with pillars extending upward at the upper reaches of the {{ill|Yakkan River|lt=Yakkan River|lt=Ekidate River|ja|駅館川|lt=Ekidate-gawa (Ukidachi River)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekidate River|qq=}} and offering hospitality. There, Emperor Jimmu commanded that Usatsuhime be given in marriage to Amenotane no Mikoto, who was a tomonomiyatsuko (ministerial retainer). The {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kujihongi|1=Kujiki}} "Kōson Hongi" (皇孫本紀) records the same event, and in its "Tennō Hongi" (天皇本紀) section, Amenotane no Mikoto is said to have delivered the words of longevity from the heavenly gods after Emperor Jimmu’s accession. According to the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|qid=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shūi|1=Kogo Shūi}}, he was commanded by Emperor Jimmu to perform the purification of heavenly and earthly sins.
The {{ill|Hiraoka Shrine|en|Hiraoka Shrine|qid=Q32422|lt=Hiraoka Shrine|1=Hiraoka Shrine}} tradition states that Amenotane no Mikoto, by the order of Emperor Jimmu, enshrined the two deities [[Ame-no-Koyane]] and {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|qid=Q22070227|lt=Himegami|1=Himegami}} on the divine peak (Kamutsudake), marking the shrine’s beginning.
== Historical Considerations ==
Although Amenotane no Mikoto is placed in the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} era, various surviving genealogies list him as a child of {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|ja|draft:Ame-no-Oshikumone|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|fr|Utilisateur:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}}.{{efn|In genealogies of the Watarai clan, Ame no Oshikumo ne no Mikoto appears under the name Ame no Murakumo no Mikoto (Ame-ni-Futakami no Mikoto, later Kobashi no Mikoto) as being involved at the time of the descent of the imperial grandson, placing him in a line separate from [[Ame no Hiwake no Mikoto]]. See <ref name="watarai">Hayakawa Junzaburō, "Watarai-shi Keizu" in 'Keizu Sōran' Vol.2, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1915, p.396.</ref>. In some lines of "Nakatomi" genealogies in the 'Sonpi Bunmyaku' and one version of the "Nakatomi-shi Sōkei", the lineage runs “{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主尊|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} - Ame no Yashimosu no Mikoto - Ame no Mitsuyori no Mikoto - Ame no Au no Mikoto - Ame no Yaoyorozu no Mikoto - Ame no Yaotomomaro no Mikoto - Tsuhaya Musubi no Mikoto - Ichichi Tamashii no Mikoto - Okoshiki Musubi no Mikoto - Ame no Koyane no Mikoto - Ame no Oshikumo no Mikoto - Amenotane no Mikoto,” whereas in the “Watarai-shi Keizu,” the line differs as recorded above.<ref name="watarai"/>|qq=}}
== Genealogy ==
His father was {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|ja|draft:Ame-no-Oshikumone|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|fr|Utilisateur:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}}, a son of [[Ame-no-Koyane]]. His wife was Usatsuhime no Mikoto, sister of {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津彦命|lt=Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 菟狭津彦命}}, and their child was Usaomi no Mikoto, ancestor of the Nakatomi (Nakatomi no Muraji).<ref>Excerpt from Shinsen Shōjiroku: "Ikare Naitō" (伊香連) entry in the second book, scroll eleven, Left Capital, Deities of Heavenly Origin (Tenshin)</ref>
== Notes ==
{{Notelist|qq=}}
{{Reflist|qq=}}
